title: Local Networking, Name Resolution, and Secure Remote Access · Command Brief: Ssh Host User Trust
summary: Restore access to a host by testing local configuration, route, name resolution, listening sockets, and SSH trust in causal order. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux ssh host user trust increment in a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate server host identity, user authentication, authorization, agent forwarding, and known-host decisions.
Test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Ssh Host User Trust
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ssh Host User Trust contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate server host identity, user authentication, authorization, agent forwarding, and known-host decisions.
